speaker: PROLOGUE
text: In Troy there lies the scene. From isles of Greece

speaker: PROLOGUE
text: The princes orgulous, their high blood chafed,

speaker: PROLOGUE
text: Have to the port of Athens sent their ships

speaker: PROLOGUE
text: Fraught with the ministers and instruments

speaker: PROLOGUE
text: Of cruel war. Sixty and nine, that wore

speaker: PROLOGUE
text: Their crownets regal, from th’ Athenian bay

speaker: PROLOGUE
text: Put forth toward Phrygia, and their vow is made

speaker: PROLOGUE
text: To ransack Troy, within whose strong immures

speaker: PROLOGUE
text: The ravished Helen, Menelaus’ queen,

speaker: PROLOGUE
text: With wanton Paris sleeps; and that’s the quarrel.

speaker: PROLOGUE
text: To Tenedos they come,

speaker: PROLOGUE
text: And the deep-drawing barks do there disgorge

speaker: PROLOGUE
text: Their warlike fraughtage. Now on Dardan plains

speaker: PROLOGUE
text: The fresh and yet unbruisèd Greeks do pitch

speaker: PROLOGUE
text: Their brave pavilions. Priam’s six-gated city—

speaker: PROLOGUE
text: Dardan and Timbria, Helias, Chetas, Troien,

speaker: PROLOGUE
text: And Antenorides—with massy staples

speaker: PROLOGUE
text: And corresponsive and fulfilling bolts,

speaker: PROLOGUE
text: Spar up the sons of Troy.

speaker: PROLOGUE
text: Now expectation, tickling skittish spirits

speaker: PROLOGUE
text: On one and other side, Trojan and Greek,

speaker: PROLOGUE
text: Sets all on hazard. And hither am I come,

speaker: PROLOGUE
text: A prologue armed, but not in confidence

speaker: PROLOGUE
text: Of author’s pen or actor’s voice, but suited

speaker: PROLOGUE
text: In like conditions as our argument,

speaker: PROLOGUE
text: To tell you, fair beholders, that our play

speaker: PROLOGUE
text: Leaps o’er the vaunt and firstlings of those broils,

speaker: PROLOGUE
text: Beginning in the middle, starting thence away

speaker: PROLOGUE
text: To what may be digested in a play.

speaker: PROLOGUE
text: Like, or find fault; do as your pleasures are.

speaker: PROLOGUE
text: Now, good or bad, ’tis but the chance of war.
